findings.evidence: `nl -ba` shows `@[expose]` at lines 90, 95, and 100; `rg -n "\.eq_def"` shows the only new uses are `Rescale.lean:134`, `:147`, and `:160`.
findings.fix: Remove these `@[expose]` annotations and prove the rescale lemmas through existing characteristic lemmas such as `isIsotropic_iff`, `isCoisotropic_iff`, and `isLagrangian_iff`, or add named non-exposing restatements if needed.
findings.issue: `IsIsotropic`, `IsCoisotropic`, and `IsLagrangian` are newly marked `@[expose]`, leaking their bodies as public `eq_def` API just so the rescale proofs can unfold them.

findings.evidence: `Rescale.lean:53` declares `@[simp] lemma orthogonal_smul {c : R} ... : (c • B).orthogonal L = B.orthogonal L`, and its only grep hit as a consumer is the proof of `orthogonal_rescale` at `Rescale.lean:128`.
findings.fix: Make this helper `private`/local to the proof, or move it to a deliberate bilinear-form API file only if Tau Ceti intends to expose that general Mathlib-facing surface.
findings.issue: `TauCeti.LinearMap.BilinForm.orthogonal_smul` is a fully public generic bilinear-form lemma in a symplectic rescale file, broader than the Lagrangian-boundary rescale API named by the PR.

summary: The rescale lemmas are the right public API, but the PR exposes implementation details beyond the roadmap target. Keep the predicate bodies and generic bilinear-form helper out of the public surface.
